name: Ulava Charu
steps: Soak horse gram overnight and pressure cook until very soft.
Strain and reserve the dark cooking stock; grind a little of the cooked gram and add back to thicken.
Simmer the stock with tamarind water, chilli powder, coriander powder and salt until it thickens to a glossy dark charu.
Crackle mustard, cumin and curry leaves in oil with onion and tomato, and add to the simmering charu.
Reduce until spoon-coating thick and serve with hot rice and a swirl of cream.
